title: Module 1
content:
Introduction-definition of instrumentation-concept of an instrument
Basic principles of measurements - various elements in a measuring
instrument - Sensing element, transducing element manipulating element
and functioning element, etc- Principles of working with a suitable
example, Classification methods of measurements, static and dynamic
characteristics  of  measuring  instrument.  Simple  problems  with
characteristics

title: Module 2
content:
Temperature measurements- temperature scales.
Basic principles and working with a neat sketch of thermometers-
mercury in glass thermometers, bimetallic thermometers resistance
temperature detectors, thermistors, thermocouples, optical pyrometers,
radiation pyrometers, Ranges of different types of temperature measuring
instruments, sources of errors and precautions to be taken in temperature
measurements.

title: Module 3
content:
Pressure measurement - basic principles and working with neat sketch
of Manometers, Bourdon gauge, Bellow element, and diaphragm element,
Pressure to electrical transducers- different types.
Vacuum measurement - Different types. basic principles and working
with neat sketch of McLeod gauge, Knudsen gauge, radioactive vacuum
gauge, thermal conductivity gauges, ionization gauge High pressure
measuring instruments.
Level measurement - direct type and indirect type. Differential pressure
method for pressurized vessels. Conductivity meters. Solid level detectors

title: Module 4
content:
Flow measurements - Liquid and gas flow measurements- list of different
types of measuring liquids and gas flow, Basic principles, and working
with a neat sketch of quantity meters, Coriolis meter, gas meters, magnetic
flow meters, variable area meters, rotameters. turbine meters, weirs
&notches, head meters, pitot tube, orifice meters &venturi meters. pH
measurement using a calomel electrode.
Composition analysis- list of methods with their advantages and
limitations.
(spectroscopic
methods,
chromatography
methods,
conductivity methods).
Developments of P&I, diagram for flow systems, level, PH control temp
control, Heat exchangers, Distillation column, CSTR, PFR, Single effect
evaporator.
